title: Asking for Directions
description: Learn how to ask for and understand basic directions in Dutch.

content.text: Welcome! In this lesson, you will learn how to navigate Dutch streets. The Netherlands is a very walkable country, so knowing directions is essential!

content.title: Giving Directions with 'Eerst... dan...'
content.explanation:
To give step-by-step directions, use 'eerst' (first) and 'dan' (then):

Eerst rechtdoor, dan links. = First straight ahead, then left.
Eerst links, dan rechts. = First left, then right.
Eerst rechts, dan rechtdoor. = First right, then straight ahead.

You can chain multiple steps together!

content.title: Quick Practice: Ask for Directions
content.expected_concept: The user should greet back and ask where something is using 'Waar is...?' followed by a place. For example: 'Goedemorgen! Waar is het station?'

content.title: Finding the Train Station
content.setting: You are in the city and need to find the train station. Ask a local for help.

conversation_flow.title: Greet and ask 'Waar is het station?'
Say 'Dank u wel!'
conversation_flow.chatbot_message: Het station? Eerst rechtdoor, dan links.

content.title: Quick Practice: You Are Lost
content.expected_concept: The user should say they are lost using 'Ik ben verdwaald' and ask where something is using 'Waar is...?'

content.title: Finding a Museum
content.setting: You are lost and want to find the Rijksmuseum. Ask a local for help.

conversation_flow.title: Say you are lost and ask where the Rijksmuseum is
Ask 'Is het ver?'
conversation_flow.chatbot_message: Het Rijksmuseum? Eerst rechtdoor, dan rechts.

content.text: Goed gedaan! You can now ask for directions and understand basic responses in Dutch. In the next lesson, we will navigate the train station!
